before_code:
have : (E hk : ℍ → ℂ) = 0 := funext fun z ↦ by simp [h]
rw [this, qExpansion_zero]; simp
description: Use simp to rewrite-and-close in one step. Instead of constructing an intermediate have with funext then rw then simp, try simp [h, lemma] directly. If simp can see the hypothesis and the rewrite lemma, it often handles the whole chain.

before_code: ext n; simp [Periodic.qParam, ofReal_one, div_one]; ring
description: Use grind over ext; simp; ring. When a goal needs structural decomposition (like funext) followed by simplification and ring arithmetic, grind [hint1, hint2] with simp-lemma hints can replace the manual ext n; simp [...]; ring pattern.

before_code: simp only [if_true, pow_zero, mul_one, Nat.succ_ne_zero, not_false_eq_true, if_false]
description: Use ↓reduceIte in simp sets for decidable if-expressions (like if n + 1 = 0 then ...) instead of manually listing if_true, if_false, Nat.succ_ne_zero, not_false_eq_true. Also ite_mul and one_mul help distribute multiplication over if.

Use simp_rw for coercion rewrites under binders. simp_rw [zpow_natCast] at this cleanly handles zpow→npow conversion inside tsum/sum binders, avoiding verbose tsum_congr fun ⟨n, _⟩ => by rw [zpow_natCast].
Don't unfold set variables before ring. If you used set β := expr, you don't need simp only [β] before ring — ring and grind handle set variables automatically.
